---
title: "CodeBook Lab"
date: "2026"
date-format: "YYYY"
codelink: "https://github.com/LorcanMcLaren/codebook-lab"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: article
    template-partials:
      - "../partials/title-block.html"
---

CodeBook Lab is an LLM annotation pipeline for computational social science. It is designed to help researchers run, compare, and evaluate annotation experiments without writing custom annotation infrastructure for each new project.

Used alongside [CodeBook Studio](codebook-studio.qmd), it allows researchers to define an annotation task once and then use that same codebook plus a human-annotated `ground-truth.csv` to run local or HPC-based LLM workflows. Human annotations serve as the validation benchmark, and CodeBook Lab strips the codebook-defined label columns from `ground-truth.csv` before sending text to the LLM, making it possible to compare model outputs against a researcher-defined gold standard.

## How Studio and Lab Fit Together

The relationship between the two tools is straightforward: [CodeBook Studio](codebook-studio.qmd) is where the annotation task is defined, while CodeBook Lab is where the LLM experiment is run and evaluated.

```{=html}
<style>
.codebook-flow {
  --codebook-border: #7a5a65;
  --codebook-card-bg: #f7f1ec;
  --codebook-data-bg: #fcf8f5;
  --codebook-title: #5f3b4a;
  --codebook-text: #4a3a40;
  --codebook-code-bg: rgba(95, 59, 74, 0.08);
  --codebook-code-border: rgba(122, 90, 101, 0.22);
  --codebook-shadow: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: minmax(220px, 1fr) 80px minmax(220px, 1fr);
  gap: 1.25rem;
  align-items: center;
  margin: 1.5rem 0 1rem 0;
}

.quarto-dark .codebook-flow {
  --codebook-border: #7b9bb5;
  --codebook-card-bg: #253544;
  --codebook-data-bg: #1f2d3a;
  --codebook-title: #b7d0e4;
  --codebook-text: #d7e6f3;
  --codebook-code-bg: #13212d;
  --codebook-code-border: rgba(183, 208, 228, 0.18);
  --codebook-shadow: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.22);
}

.codebook-card {
  border: 2px solid var(--codebook-border);
  border-radius: 12px;
  background: var(--codebook-card-bg);
  color: var(--codebook-text);
  padding: 1rem 1.1rem;
  box-shadow: 0 2px 8px var(--codebook-shadow);
}

.codebook-card h3 {
  margin: 0 0 0.6rem 0;
  font-size: 1.15rem;
  color: var(--codebook-title);
}

.codebook-card ul {
  margin: 0;
  padding-left: 1.1rem;
}

.codebook-card li {
  margin-bottom: 0.35rem;
  color: var(--codebook-text);
}

.codebook-arrow {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: center;
  align-items: center;
  color: var(--codebook-title);
  font-size: 2rem;
  font-weight: 700;
}

.codebook-bridge {
  grid-column: 1 / -1;
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr 80px 1fr;
  gap: 1.25rem;
  align-items: center;
  margin-top: -0.25rem;
}

.codebook-data {
  border: 1.5px dashed var(--codebook-border);
  border-radius: 10px;
  background: var(--codebook-data-bg);
  padding: 0.85rem 1rem;
  color: var(--codebook-text);
}

.codebook-data strong {
  color: var(--codebook-title);
}

.codebook-card code,
.codebook-card li code,
.codebook-data code {
  color: var(--codebook-text);
  background: var(--codebook-code-bg);
  border: 1px solid var(--codebook-code-border);
  border-radius: 0.45rem;
  padding: 0.08rem 0.35rem;
}

.quarto-dark .codebook-card code,
.quarto-dark .codebook-card li code,
.quarto-dark .codebook-data code {
  color: var(--codebook-text) !important;
  background: var(--codebook-code-bg) !important;
  border-color: var(--codebook-code-border) !important;
}

@media (max-width: 900px) {
  .codebook-flow,
  .codebook-bridge {
    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  }

  .codebook-arrow {
    transform: rotate(90deg);
  }
}
</style>

<div class="codebook-flow">
  <div class="codebook-card">
    <h3>CodeBook Studio</h3>
    <ul>
      <li>Define the annotation task</li>
      <li>Annotate texts with humans</li>
      <li>Export <code>codebook.json</code></li>
    </ul>
  </div>

  <div class="codebook-arrow" aria-hidden="true">→</div>

  <div class="codebook-card">
    <h3>CodeBook Lab</h3>
    <ul>
      <li>Run LLM annotation experiments</li>
      <li>Compare models, prompts, and settings</li>
      <li>Evaluate outputs against human labels</li>
    </ul>
  </div>

  <div class="codebook-bridge">
    <div class="codebook-data">
      <strong>Outputs from Studio</strong><br>
      <code>codebook.json</code> and a human-annotated <code>ground-truth.csv</code>
    </div>

    <div class="codebook-arrow" aria-hidden="true">→</div>

    <div class="codebook-data">
      <strong>Evaluation in Lab</strong><br>
      Accuracy, agreement, runtime, energy, and emissions tradeoffs
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<p><em>CodeBook Studio defines the task; CodeBook Lab runs and evaluates the experiment.</em></p>
```

CodeBook Lab supports the main annotation types commonly used in text-as-data workflows:

- Binary labels
- Categorical labels
- Likert-scale ordinal labels
- Open-ended text responses

The pipeline is designed to make it easier to compare tradeoffs across:

- Models available through Ollama
- Prompt styles and example use
- Hyperparameter settings such as temperature and top-p
- Runtime, energy use, and estimated carbon emissions

The repository includes a synthetic starter task and a configuration-driven workflow so that most experiments can be controlled through a single `param_grid.yaml` file rather than by editing pipeline code.
